news.title: Meta's court losses spell potential trouble for AI research, consumer safety
news.summary: Meta's two courtroom defeats centered on different cases but both involved allegations that the company knew about its products' harms. Meta was found guilty in both cases but the verdicts were overturned on appeal.

news.title: Eli Lilly reaches $2.75 billion deal with Insilico to bring AI-developed drugs to the global market
news.summary: U.S. pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly will give Hong Kong-listed Insilico $115 million upfront to bring some of its AI-discovered drugs to the global market.

news.title: Bahrain aluminum giant says Iranian attack targeted its facility
news.summary: Aluminium Bahrain, or Alba, hosts the world's largest smelter. The attack adds to the pressure on global supplies of the metal.

news.title: New fees, fewer flights: Higher fuel prices pinch consumer budgets beyond the gas pump
news.summary: As corporate policies change, Americans will feel higher oil prices in their wallets beyond the gas pump. As corporate policies changed, Americans would feel higherOil prices in the wallets.

news.title: Top Wall Street analysts like these dividend stocks for solid returns
news.summary: These stocks that pay dividends consistently have the ability to generate long-term capital appreciation. These stocks pay dividend consistently and pay a high rate of return on invested capital.

news.title: My brother says lawyers can get him a Medicaid nursing home in Florida for a ‘hefty fee,’ despite his assets. Is this a scam?
news.summary: “I do not believe there is a way to preserve assets and still qualify for Medicaid,” he says. “I don’t believe there’s a way.”

news.title: ‘Several hurricanes have come close’: I’m 73 and live in a mobile home in Florida. Do I ditch my $2,400 home insurance?
news.summary: “I live in a 2016 double-wide mobile home with no mortgage,” she says. “I’m a single mother of two. I’ve never had a mortgage.”

news.title: An older relative wants to give my daughter $19,000 when she turns 18. I said no. Who’s right?
news.summary: “We don’t think it’s healthy for very young adults to have access to large sums of money without working for it,” he says.

news.title: ‘This guy has no manners’: My Airbnb guest requested I buy bacon and beer. The $30 bill remains unpaid. Do I insist?
news.summary: “I don’t want him to retaliate with a bad review,” she says. “I’m not going to let him get away with it.”

news.title: ‘I’m completely gobsmacked’: My elderly brother has a reverse mortgage — yet he still ran out of money. Do I help?
news.summary: “We simply can’t afford to take on his financial situation,” she said. “My husband and I are both retired and have saved for years.”
